text:
hey gang! I've been a podcast listener and slack-lurker for a long time..and just got back from the meetup in SF. I live in Oakland, CA and work in business intelligence at an enterprise software company. My current title is Analytics Engineer and I work mostly in SQL, but identify foremost as a "data person" and got in fits of about when i feel like (and want to feel like) a "real" engineer.

FOC-wise, no real personal projects, but I'm interested in good tools for doing data work (the R library dplyr for piped data wrangling is the technology I love must purely), as well as the best ways to build a bunch of janky little word games. A thing that's been on my mind lately is genres/things that cross physical and digital (mostly in the context of playing the word game Boggle IRL versus playing online).

I cringe if I watch myself, but I am ultimately proud of this 15m talk I gave a while back called <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PpAfmTgcGPY|"Charts As Utterances">, applying Grice's maxims to data visualization (before I started working in the software industry, I made it two years into a phd linguistics program). A few years back when Ivan ran a FOC community survey, I made an <https://observablehq.com/@a-lexwein/what-future-of-coding-topics-interest-you-most|Observable notebook> to visualize overlap in a question about interests. <https://alex-we.in/posts/2025-04-06-comics-about-comics.md|This blog post> I wrote this year on Comics about Comics might be interesting to some folks here.
